summary: Code verification passed syntactically but the run() function is missing entirely, making the script non-executable as a standalone module.

critical_issues.issue: run() function is never defined but is called in __main__ block
critical_issues.recommendation: Define a run() function that executes the full workflow: build_network -> create_citylearn_env -> build_building_bus_map -> create_citylearn_agent -> run_citylearn -> run_grid (before redistribution), then computes voltage sensitivity, redistributes buildings, reruns, compares KPIs, and saves outputs.

critical_issues.issue: Voltage sensitivity analysis and building redistribution logic are completely absent
critical_issues.location: Missing - no function or code block exists for this
critical_issues.recommendation: After first run_grid call, compute per-bus voltage std or max deviation from vm_ts, identify high-sensitivity buses, reassign buildings away from those buses in building_bus_map, then call run_grid again with the new map.

critical_issues.issue: KPI comparison and plot saving between before/after redistribution are not implemented
critical_issues.recommendation: Call evaluate_grid_kpis on both grid_results_before and grid_results_after, merge the two KPI DataFrames, save to CSV, and generate matplotlib comparison bar plots saved via plt.savefig to DATA_DIR.

code_patches.replacement:
def run():
    net = build_network(grid_model="pandapower", case_name="case33bw")
    env = create_citylearn_env(dataset=None, central=True, pp_net=net, building_number=25)
    building_bus_map = build_building_bus_map(env, net, slack_bus=0)
    agent = create_citylearn_agent(env, strategy="RBC", episodes=1)
    building_kw = run_citylearn(env, agent)
    grid_results_before = run_grid(building_kw, net, building_bus_map, plot=True, save=True)
    kpis_before = evaluate_grid_kpis(grid_results_before, save=False)
    kpis_before.to_csv(os.path.join(DATA_DIR, "grid_kpis_before.csv"), index=False)
    with open(os.path.join(DATA_DIR, "building_bus_map_before.json"), "w") as f:
        json.dump({str(k): v for k, v in building_bus_map.items()}, f, indent=2)
    vm_ts = np.asarray(grid_results_before["vm_ts"])
    available_buses = [int(b) for b in net["model"].bus.index if int(b) != 0]
    bus_voltage_std = np.std(vm_ts, axis=0)
    n_buses = len(available_buses)
    bus_std_map = {available_buses[i % n_buses]: bus_voltage_std[i] if i < len(bus_voltage_std) else 0.0 for i in range(n_buses)}
    sensitivity_df = pd.DataFrame({"bus": list(bus_std_map.keys()), "voltage_std": list(bus_std_map.values())}).sort_values("voltage_std", ascending=False)
    sensitivity_df.to_csv(os.path.join(DATA_DIR, "voltage_sensitivity.csv"), index=False)
    high_sensitivity_buses = set(sensitivity_df.head(max(1, n_buses // 4))["bus"].tolist())
    low_sensitivity_buses = [b for b in available_buses if b not in high_sensitivity_buses]
    if not low_sensitivity_buses:
        low_sensitivity_buses = available_buses
    new_building_bus_map = {i: low_sensitivity_buses[i % len(low_sensitivity_buses)] for i in range(len(env.unwrapped.buildings))}
    env.unwrapped.building_bus_map = dict(new_building_bus_map)
    with open(os.path.join(DATA_DIR, "building_bus_map_after.json"), "w") as f:
        json.dump({str(k): v for k, v in new_building_bus_map.items()}, f, indent=2)
    env2 = create_citylearn_env(dataset=None, central=True, pp_net=net, building_number=25)
    env2.unwrapped.building_bus_map = dict(new_building_bus_map)
    agent2 = create_citylearn_agent(env2, strategy="RBC", episodes=1)
    building_kw2 = run_citylearn(env2, agent2)
    grid_results_after = run_grid(building_kw2, net, new_building_bus_map, plot=False, save=False)
    kpis_after = evaluate_grid_kpis(grid_results_after, save=False)
    kpis_after.to_csv(os.path.join(DATA_DIR, "grid_kpis_after.csv"), index=False)
    merged = kpis_before.merge(kpis_after, on=["category", "metric", "unit"], suffixes=("_before", "_after"))
    merged.to_csv(os.path.join(DATA_DIR, "grid_kpis_comparison.csv"), index=False)
    voltage_kpis = merged[merged["category"] == "voltage"].copy()
    if not voltage_kpis.empty:
        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12, 6))
        x = np.arange(len(voltage_kpis))
        width = 0.35
        ax.bar(x - width/2, voltage_kpis["value_before"].astype(float), width, label="Before")
        ax.bar(x + width/2, voltage_kpis["value_after"].astype(float), width, label="After")
        ax.set_xticks(x)
        ax.set_xticklabels(voltage_kpis["metric"].tolist(), rotation=45, ha="right")
        ax.set_title("Voltage KPI Comparison Before vs After Redistribution")
        ax.legend()
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.savefig(os.path.join(DATA_DIR, "kpi_comparison_voltage.png"))
        plt.close()
    vm_before = np.asarray(grid_results_before["vm_ts"])
    vm_after = np.asarray(grid_results_after["vm_ts"])
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 5))
    axes[0].hist(vm_before.flatten(), bins=50, color="steelblue", edgecolor="k")
    axes[0].set_title("Voltage Distribution Before")
    axes[0].set_xlabel("Voltage [p.u.]")
    axes[1].hist(vm_after.flatten(), bins=50, color="darkorange", edgecolor="k")
    axes[1].set_title("Voltage Distribution After")
    axes[1].set_xlabel("Voltage [p.u.]")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(os.path.join(DATA_DIR, "voltage_distribution_comparison.png"))
    plt.close()
    print("[Done] All outputs saved to", DATA_DIR)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    run()
